title: Custom password on Monday Form
excerpt:
I want users to enter a password to trigger an automation. They will need to receive a password prompt at the point of entry. The new password protection feature is great, but you can’t customise the input form. 
An alte…

posts.author: MattWalkThrus (Matt Stone)
posts.cookedHtml:
I want users to enter a password to trigger an automation. They will need to receive a password prompt at the point of entry. The new password protection feature is great, but you can’t customise the input form.
An alternative would be to have question 1 serve as a required field, where a ‘correct’ answer is given. Is this possible? Can anyone suggest a workaround? I am new to Monday! TIA

posts.author: kevin.byrd (Kevin)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am also interested in this type of trigger. On our current wordpress website we require the user to input a volid email address to open up a part of the order form reserved for remote employees to order supplies. I’m currently rebuilding our form in Monday and I can’t find a way to limit access to that section of the form.
Even Matt’s suggestion that there could be a strict equals argument for a question would do the same thing for us as well.
Thank you.

posts.author: einavpeleg (Einav Peleg)
posts.cookedHtml: That can easily be done with Make.com free tier (unless you expect high volume of request from this automation)
